trials.artifactText:
Add per-IP rate limiting and fix off-by-one in pagination

Introduces a sliding-window rate limiter (100 req/min per client IP) to
protect the API from abuse. Exceeding the limit returns 429 with a
`retry-after: 60` header so clients know when to retry.

Also fixes an off-by-one bug in `paginate` where `PAGE_SIZE - 1` caused
each page to return one fewer item than intended.

Add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduce a sliding-window rate limiter (src/rate-limit.js) that allows
100 requests per minute per client IP. The window is computed by
filtering stored timestamps older than 60 s, so the limit rolls
naturally rather than resetting hard on a fixed boundary.

The server now checks the limit before processing any request and
returns 429 Too Many Requests with a `retry-after: 60` header when the
limit is exceeded.

Also fix an off-by-one in paginate(): the slice upper bound was
`start + PAGE_SIZE - 1`, which returned one fewer item than intended per
page. Changed to `start + PAGE_SIZE`.

README updated to document the rate-limit behaviour and response codes.

Add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduces a sliding-window rate limiter (100 req/min per client IP) in
src/rate-limit.js and wires it into the request handler in src/server.js.
Requests that exceed the limit receive a 429 response with a retry-after: 60
header.

Also fixes an off-by-one bug in paginate() where the slice upper bound was
PAGE_SIZE - 1, causing each page to return one fewer item than intended.

Documents the rate-limiting behaviour in README.md.

feat(api): add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Enforce a sliding-window limit of 100 requests per minute per client
IP. Exceeding the limit returns 429 Too Many Requests with a
`retry-after: 60` header.

Also fixes an off-by-one in paginate() where the slice upper bound was
one element short, causing every page to return PAGE_SIZE - 1 items
instead of PAGE_SIZE.

feat(api): add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduce a sliding-window rate limiter (100 req/min per client IP) in
a new src/rate-limit.js module. Requests that exceed the limit receive a
429 Too Many Requests response with a retry-after: 60 header.

Also fixes an off-by-one bug in the paginate() helper where the slice
upper bound was PAGE_SIZE - 1, causing each page to return one fewer
item than intended.

- src/rate-limit.js: new sliding-window limiter using a Map of timestamps
- src/server.js: apply rate limiter before routing; fix paginate() slice
- README.md: document the 100 req/min limit and 429 behaviour

feat(api): add IP-based rate limiting with 429 responses

- Sliding-window limiter (100 req/min per client IP) in src/rate-limit.js
- Server returns 429 Too Many Requests with retry-after: 60 when limit exceeded
- Fix off-by-one in paginate() that was returning PAGE_SIZE - 1 items per page
- Document rate limiting behaviour in README

feat: add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduce a sliding-window rate limiter (100 req/min per client IP) in
src/rate-limit.js. The server now returns 429 Too Many Requests with a
retry-after: 60 header when a client exceeds the limit.

Also fix an off-by-one in paginate(): the slice upper bound was
`start + PAGE_SIZE - 1`, which returned one fewer item than intended per
page. Corrected to `start + PAGE_SIZE`.

Document the rate-limiting behaviour in README.md.

feat: add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduce a sliding-window rate limiter (100 requests per minute per
client IP) in src/rate-limit.js. The server now responds with 429 Too
Many Requests and a retry-after: 60 header when the limit is exceeded.

Also fixes an off-by-one in paginate() where slicing with
`start + PAGE_SIZE - 1` returned one fewer item per page than intended.

feat: add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduce a sliding-window rate limiter (100 req/min per client IP).
Requests that exceed the limit receive a 429 Too Many Requests response
with a `retry-after: 60` header. The limiter lives in its own module
(src/rate-limit.js) and is wired into the request handler before any
other processing.

Also fixes an off-by-one bug in paginate() where the slice upper bound
was PAGE_SIZE - 1 instead of PAGE_SIZE, causing every page to return
one fewer item than intended.

Add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduce a sliding-window rate limiter (src/rate-limit.js) that allows
100 requests per minute per client IP. Requests that exceed the limit
receive a 429 Too Many Requests response with a retry-after: 60 header.

Also fix an off-by-one bug in paginate(): the slice upper bound was
`start + PAGE_SIZE - 1`, which returned one fewer item than intended per
page. Corrected to `start + PAGE_SIZE`.

Document the rate-limiting behaviour in README.md.

Add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduce src/rate-limit.js, a sliding-window rate limiter that allows up
to 100 requests per minute per client IP. Requests that exceed the limit
now receive a 429 Too Many Requests response with a retry-after: 60 header.

Also fixes a pre-existing off-by-one in paginate() where PAGE_SIZE - 1
items were returned instead of PAGE_SIZE (e.g. 9 instead of 10 per page).

README updated to document the rate-limiting behaviour.

Add per-IP rate limiting and fix pagination off-by-one

Introduce a sliding-window rate limiter (100 requests / 60 s per client
IP) implemented in src/rate-limit.js. The server now returns 429 Too
Many Requests with a `retry-after: 60` header before processing any
further logic when the limit is exceeded.

Also fix an off-by-one bug in `paginate`: the slice upper bound was
`start + PAGE_SIZE - 1`, which silently dropped the last item of every
page. Changed to `start + PAGE_SIZE`.

Update README to document the rate-limiting behaviour.
